Name: Zero-Knowledge Infrastructure
Summary: Universal privacy + compliance compatibility creates massive market opportunity
Full Description: ZK proofs are the only known technology allowing verification without trust, privacy without central authority, compliance without surveillance. This directly addresses Vitalik's concern that 'with modern AI to interpret it, there may be no place to hide.' ZK enables selective disclosure - proving claims without revealing private information.
D/acc Alignment: 5 - Perfect alignment; enables defense without centralization
Market Opportunity: $90B ZK L2 market by 2031 (60.7% CAGR); $7.59B ZK tech by 2033 (22.1% CAGR); Institutional adoption accelerating (Deutsche Bank, HSBC, Walmart exploring)
Key Investment Vectors: zkEVM Rollups, ZK Credential Systems, ZK Proving Infrastructure, ZK Developer Tooling
Risk Factors: Technical complexity; proving time optimization; developer adoption curve; competition among implementations
Validation Needed: Enterprise ZK pilots (Deutsche Bank/HSBC) converting to production deployment

Name: Decentralized Identity & Attestation
Summary: Foundation for institutional adoption while preserving user sovereignty
Full Description: Creates self-sovereign alternatives to platform-controlled credentials. Enables individuals to own and selectively disclose identity without depending on corporations or governments. Financial institutions report 80% compliance cost reduction and 90% faster verification.
Market Opportunity: Multi-trillion addressable market across finance, healthcare, governance; 200+ DID methods published; Buenos Aires deployed ZK identity for 3.6M citizens
Key Investment Vectors: Attestation Protocols (EAS ecosystem), ZK Credential Systems, Reputation Aggregation, Social Recovery Solutions
Risk Factors: Adoption chicken-and-egg problem; standards fragmentation; key recovery UX challenges
Validation Needed: Buenos Aires ZK identity replicability; institutional DID adoption rates

Name: Privacy-Preserving Computation (FHE/MPC)
Summary: Institutional privacy gap creates demand-supply mismatch
Full Description: Enables computation on encrypted data from multiple parties - more composable than ZK for multi-party scenarios. Directly addresses critical gap between institutional privacy requirements and on-chain transparency. FHE represents 'the final frontier of on-chain privacy.'
D/acc Alignment: 5 - Prevents AI systems from accessing raw information
Market Opportunity: Only ~$250M total funding vs multi-trillion DeFi/RWA opportunity; Zama unicorn status validates timing; current 20 TPS targeting 1000 TPS with ASICs
Key Investment Vectors: FHE Smart Contract Platforms, MPC Wallet Infrastructure, FHE Hardware Acceleration, Privacy-Preserving Compliance
Risk Factors: Current 20 TPS performance limitation; ASIC development timeline uncertainty; nascent ecosystem
Validation Needed: FHE performance trajectory (20 TPS → 1000 TPS) timeline validation

Name: Data Availability Layers
Summary: Infrastructure bottleneck removal for rollup scaling
Full Description: Decentralizes critical DA bottleneck for rollup scaling. Celestia's Data Availability Sampling enables light node verification without downloading full blocks - preventing validator centralization. DA costs represent 90%+ of L2 operating expenses; Celestia demonstrated 99.9% cost reduction.
Market Opportunity: Market scales directly with rollup adoption; three well-funded competitors creating validated market (Celestia $155M, EigenDA $170M+, Avail $27M)
Key Investment Vectors: DA Layer Native Tokens, Rollup Infrastructure Built on DA Layers
Risk Factors: Competition creates execution risk; winner-take-most dynamics possible; native token economics vary significantly
Validation Needed: Celestia cost advantage translating to sustained rollup adoption vs EigenDA Ethereum-aligned security

Name: Quadratic/Retroactive Funding Infrastructure
Summary: Democratic coordination primitive demonstrating sustainable public goods financing
Full Description: Mathematically amplifies small contributions, enabling collective prioritization of public goods without centralized allocation. 400x matching multipliers for small donations demonstrated. Gitcoin distributed $60M+; Optimism RetroPGF committed $100M+ to 501 contributors.
D/acc Alignment: 4 - Enables democratic coordination without central authority
Market Opportunity: $60M+ distributed via Gitcoin; $100M+ committed by Optimism; infrastructure layer (Allo Protocol) more investable than individual funding rounds
Key Investment Vectors: Funding Protocol Infrastructure (Allo), Sybil Resistance Systems, Impact Verification (Hypercerts)
Risk Factors: Sybil attack vulnerability; funding predictability challenges; adoption beyond crypto-native communities
Validation Needed: QV adoption (100+ DAOs) correlation with improved participation rates vs baseline 17%

Name: Decentralized Stablecoin Mechanisms
Summary: Reduce concentration risk in the largest crypto market segment
Full Description: 91% of $309B stablecoin market remains centralized and fiat-backed, creating concentration risk. Decentralized alternatives preserve utility while maintaining decentralization. Ethena's USDe grew from $146M to $6.2B via delta-neutral strategies.
D/acc Alignment: 4 - Reduces single points of failure in monetary infrastructure
Market Opportunity: $309B market cap; $27.6T annual volume exceeding Visa+Mastercard; regulatory pressure on centralized stablecoins creating opportunity
Key Investment Vectors: Decentralized Stablecoin Protocols, Yield Optimization Layers, Delta-Neutral Mechanisms
Risk Factors: Historical peg stability failures (algorithmic designs); regulatory uncertainty; centralized stablecoin network effects
Validation Needed: Sustained peg stability under stress; regulatory clarity on decentralized mechanisms

Name: Oracle Infrastructure (Emerging Networks)
Summary: Critical middleware with decentralization trajectory opportunity
Full Description: Critical middleware for real-world integration but Chainlink's 46-58% market share creates d/acc tension. Emerging networks with more decentralized architecture may capture share. Pyth grew 46x in 2024; Chronicle offers 61% cheaper operation.
Market Opportunity: All sectors requiring off-chain data (insurance, supply chain, DeFi, carbon markets); Pyth 46x growth validates competition
Key Investment Vectors: Emerging Oracle Networks with Decentralized Architecture (Pyth, Chronicle, RedStone)
Risk Factors: Network effects favoring incumbents; integration switching costs; security requirements
Validation Needed: Market share dynamics; decentralization improvements in emerging networks

Name: ZK-Based Cross-Chain Infrastructure
Summary: Security resolution for cross-chain with reduced trust assumptions
Full Description: Necessary for ecosystem connectivity but $2.8B historical exploit losses (40% of Web3 hacks) and centralization tendencies create caution. ZK-based bridges may resolve security concerns with validity proofs instead of trust assumptions.
D/acc Alignment: 3 - ZK variants offer higher alignment than trusted bridges
Market Opportunity: Essential infrastructure as multi-chain world develops; LayerZero V2 DVNs enabling ZK verification; intent-based systems growing
Key Investment Vectors: ZK-Based Bridges (LayerZero V2 DVNs), Intent-Based Systems with Decentralized Solvers (CoW Protocol)
Risk Factors: Bridge exploit history ($2.8B+ losses); security track record; implementation complexity
Validation Needed: ZK bridge security in production environments; solver decentralization
